commit.message:
fix: snapshot ORM scalars before UoW commits to prevent MissingGreenlet (#1588)

In async SQLAlchemy, expire_on_commit=True (default) expires all ORM
attributes after a UoW commit. Accessing them afterwards triggers a
synchronous lazy-load which raises MissingGreenlet.

Snapshot all ORM scalars (email, name, auth0_id, auth0_org_id) into
local variables while the session is still active, before any UoW
that might commit. Affected methods:

- operations.create_organization: owner_user.email after _create_local
- service.remove_member: org.name/auth0_org_id after membership delete
- service.change_member_role: target_user.email, org.name after role update
- provisioning.sync_user_organizations: user.email in except handler

commit.message:
fix: remove db.refresh that triggers delete-orphan on UserOrganization (#1585)

db.refresh(local_org) after flush caused SQLAlchemy to reload the
Organization with noload on user_organizations, yielding an empty
collection. Combined with cascade='all, delete-orphan', this silently
deleted the just-created UserOrganization record on the next flush/commit.

All scalar attributes (id, created_at, modified_at) use Python-side
defaults so they are already populated after flush — no refresh needed.

tree 4130d3a29a3e6da257da9b88ff20a1e638d66bfe

defaults so they are already populated after flush — no refresh needed.

commit.message:
fix: paginate Auth0 get_user_organizations and get_organization_members (#1587)

Auth0 Management API returns max 50 results by default. Users with
50+ org memberships had their beyond-page-1 memberships silently
deleted by sync_user_organizations Pass 2 on every login.

Add _get_all_pages helper that iterates through pages (per_page=100)
and apply it to both get_user_organizations and get_organization_members.

commit.message:
Hotfix/user refresh selectinload (#1586)

* fix: use selectinload in UserRepository.refresh to prevent 422 on login

UserRepository.refresh() used bare db.refresh() which only reloads scalar
columns, leaving noload relationships (user_organizations -> organization)
unpopulated. After sync_user_organizations committed its UoW and called
refresh, Pydantic serialization failed with 422 for every organization.

- Replace db.refresh() with an explicit selectinload query matching the
  CRUD layer _get_user_query_with_orgs() pattern
- Harden _handle_existing_user fallback to re-fetch the user on failure
  instead of reusing a potentially dirty ORM object

* cleanup: simplify _handle_existing_user fallback, remove redundant re-fetch

* fix: snapshot user before sync to avoid MissingGreenlet on fallback

After a failed sync the UoW expires the ORM object, so accessing
attributes in the except path raises MissingGreenlet. Serialize
the user to a Pydantic schema while still attached, use that as
the safe fallback.

commit.message:
refactor: replace getattr with direct attribute access in GitHubSource

Add class-level type annotations and use self.attr directly instead of
defensive getattr calls—attributes are always set in create().
style: render boolean config toggle inline with title and description

fix: render boolean config fields as toggles in source config UI

The SourceConfigView was rendering all config fields as text inputs.
Boolean fields (like sync_pull_requests) now render as toggle switches,
matching the existing pattern used for the OAuth credentials toggle.

feat: add merged PR and review comment syncing to GitHub connector

Adds GitHubPullRequestEntity and GitHubPRCommentEntity to sync merged
pull requests and their inline review comments. Gated behind a new
sync_pull_requests config flag (default off). Includes incremental
sync support via a dedicated last_pr_updated_at cursor field.

commit.message:
fix: update auth0_id on login instead of 409 conflict, add email verification screen (#1581)

* fix: update auth0_id on login instead of 409 conflict, remove conflict UI

Email is the source of truth for user identity. When an existing user
logs in, their auth0_id is updated to whatever Auth0 sends (Auth0
account linking guarantees a stable sub). The 409 conflict path and
the frontend Account Conflict UI are removed entirely.

Changes:
- service.py: _handle_existing_user updates auth0_id instead of raising
  ValueError; docstring updated
- users.py: removed ValueError catch / 409 response
- Callback.tsx: removed conflict error state, auto-logout timer, and
  Account Conflict UI
- test_service.py: conflict tests replaced with auth0_id update tests

* cleanup: remove has_auth0_id_conflict, add type hints to _handle_existing_user

commit.message:
fix: propagate non-RPC errors in _ensure_singleton_schedule

Bare except-pass silently swallowed unexpected errors, falling through
to schedule creation. Update test to expect propagation.
style: add missing docstring for __post_init__ (D105)

test: add table-driven tests for domains/syncs/SyncService (0% → 100%)

Cover happy path, factory error → FAILED marking, optional kwarg
forwarding, and constructor wiring.

refactor: inject repos into CreateSyncJobActivity, eliminate all crud usage

Replace lazy crud.* calls with constructor-injected repository protocols
(sync_repo, sync_job_repo, sc_repo, conn_repo, collection_repo).
Clean up _wait_for_running_jobs signature removing pass-through params.
refactor: inject EmailService into CheckAndNotifyExpiringKeysActivity
fix: add FakeSyncService and wire into test Container fixture
style: fix ruff formatting in test_temporal_schedule_service

fix: break circular import that caused container to resolve as module

SyncService import in core/container/factory.py created a cycle:
__init__.py → factory.py → SyncService → SyncFactory → container (partially loaded).
Made SyncService import lazy. Also changed platform/sync/factory.py to
use runtime module access instead of value binding to avoid capturing None.
chore: remove unnecessary future annotations and TYPE_CHECKING guards

refactor: move SyncService to domains/syncs with DI, delete core/sync_service.py

Create domains/syncs/service.py with SyncJobServiceProtocol injected via
constructor. Add SyncServiceProtocol, register in container + factory,
wire into RunSyncActivity. Eliminates last core/sync_service singleton.

refactor: inject SyncJobServiceProtocol into temporal activities

Wire SyncJobServiceProtocol into RunSyncActivity,
MarkSyncJobCancelledActivity, and CleanupStuckSyncJobsActivity via
constructor injection in wiring.py. Removes 3 lazy singleton imports.

refactor: inject SyncJobServiceProtocol into admin cancel endpoints

Replace lazy core.sync_job_service singleton imports with
Inject(SyncJobServiceProtocol) in admin_cancel_sync_job and
admin_cancel_sync_by_id.

refactor: delete dead methods from SyncService, keep only run()

8 methods (create_and_run_sync, trigger_sync_run, update_sync_schedule,
delete_sync, list_sync_jobs, get_last_sync_job, get_sync_job,
get_sync_status) were dead code — replaced by domain services. Removes
all 3 [code blue] todo lazy container imports.
fix: resolve E501 line-length violations in schedule service tests
refactor: delete dead CleanupService and related builder/context
chore: annotate lazy container imports with [code blue] todo
test: add tests for delete_schedule_handle and ensure_system_schedules
fix: add missing docstring args for injected temporal_workflow_service

refactor: kill legacy Temporal singletons, inject via protocol DI

Delete core/temporal_service.py and platform/temporal/schedule_service.py
(~1020 lines). All consumers migrated to domain protocol services:

- admin.py: Inject(TemporalWorkflowServiceProtocol) for 3 endpoints
- main.py: container.temporal_schedule_service.ensure_system_schedules()
- activities: constructor-injected protocols via wiring.py
- sync_service, cleanup/service, models/sync: lazy container access

Expand TemporalScheduleServiceProtocol with delete_schedule_handle()
and ensure_system_schedules() for activities and startup respectively.

fix: skip unknown feature flags in Organization schema validation (#1579)

The extract_enabled_features model_validator already skipped unknown flags
when hydrating from an ORM model, but returned dicts (from cache, context,
or API) unfiltered. Pydantic then rejected unknown values like
s3_destination that exist in the DB but not in FeatureFlagEnum.

Filter unknown flags in the dict path too, for both Organization and
OrganizationWithRole schemas.

commit.message:
chore: upgrade Poetry to 2.3.2

Poetry was pinned to three different versions (1.8.2,
1.8.4, and floating `latest`) across seven locations
using three different installation methods (`pip`,
`snok/install-poetry`, and `curl` installer).

Consolidate on Poetry 2.3.2 everywhere and add
`requires-poetry = ">=2.3"` to `pyproject.toml` so
Poetry itself enforces the minimum version at runtime.
CI workflows now all use `snok/install-poetry` with
the same SHA pin; Dockerfiles keep an exact pin for
reproducibility. The lock file is regenerated in the
2.1 format.

Also bumps `fern-docs.yml` and `publish-sdks-and-mcp`
Python from 3.11 to 3.13 for consistency with the
rest of the project.

commit.message:
chore: migrate to Python 3.13

The runtime, CI, and tooling all targeted 3.11 but the
migration branch had already updated CI workflows and
Dockerfiles to 3.13 without aligning everything else.
This commit completes the migration and fixes issues
introduced along the way.

Narrow the `pyproject.toml` floor from `>=3.11` to
`>=3.13` so it matches the Black, Ruff, and Mypy target
versions (`py313`/`3.13`) that were already changed.
Regenerate `poetry.lock` against the new constraint.

Re-pin all three Dockerfiles to a specific patch and
SHA-256 digest (`python:3.13.12-slim@sha256:8bc6...`)
to restore the supply-chain reproducibility that c3187742
originally established for 3.11.

Fix `delete_temporal_schedules_after_sync_delete` in
`models/sync.py`, a synchronous SQLAlchemy `after_delete`
listener that called `get_running_loop()` unconditionally.
When no loop is running the call raises `RuntimeError`,
which the outer `except Exception` swallowed silently,
leaking Temporal schedules. Apply the same try/except
pattern already used by the sibling `before_delete`
listener: attempt `get_running_loop()`, fall back to
`asyncio.run()`.

commit.message:
refactor: extract users domain service + protocolize email (#1573)

* refactor: extract users domain service + protocolize email

Move user create-or-update business logic from the endpoint into a
proper domain service layer (protocols → service → logic → fakes),
matching the organizations domain "code blue" pattern.

commit.message:
fix: close cross-tenant access via null-user bypass in context resolver (#1574)

The _validate_organization_access method in context_resolver.py used a
combined condition that silently passed when auth.user was None (valid
Auth0 token but user not in DB). Combined with X-Organization-ID header,
this allowed accessing any tenant source connection metadata.

- Split condition to check auth.method first, then reject null user with 401
- Add catch-all else clause to reject unhandled auth methods
- Add org-scoped query filters to Connection CRUD for defense in depth
- Add unit tests covering the null-user attack vector

fix: org update crash and Auth0 role assignment on admin add-self (#1571)

* fix: org update crash and Auth0 role assignment on admin add-self

- Remove modified_by_email assignment in CRUDOrganization.update()
  since Organization model does not use UserMixin (fixes 500 on org
  name change)
- Assign Auth0 role when admin joins org via /admin/.../add-self
  using existing set_organization_member_roles

* fix: re-raise Auth0 role-assignment errors instead of swallowing them

Auth0 failures in add_self_to_organization were caught and logged as
warnings, allowing the local DB membership to be created even when
Auth0 was in an inconsistent state. Now surfaces failures as HTTP
errors (500 for missing role config, 502 for Auth0 API errors) to
prevent local DB from diverging from Auth0 (source of truth).

fix: prevent unpaid orgs from getting paid limits, clean up stale billing data (#1567)

Root cause: billing_plan was set to pro/team during org creation before
Stripe Checkout completed. If checkout was abandoned, the org kept
paid limits forever. The _infer_limit fallback (added in #1552) made
this worse by trusting billing_plan without checking for an active
subscription.

Changes:
- _infer_limit now requires stripe_subscription_id before granting
  paid limits from the billing record fallback
- create_billing_record always starts as developer; real plan is set
  by the customer.subscription.created webhook after checkout
- Add checkout.session.expired handler for observability
- Data migration: downgrade 318 unpaid pro/team orgs, complete
  expired/orphaned billing periods
- Remove verbose debug logging from get_current_usage and
  increment_usage that was flooding production logs
- Fix ADDITIONAL_CORS_ORIGINS crash when value is already a list

commit.message:
fix: make Auth0 the source of truth for org membership (#1568)

Flip all org membership mutations to Auth0-first, DB-second to prevent
state drift between Auth0 and the local database. This fixes the 404
errors on member deletion and the "user resurrection" bug where removed
members reappear on next login.

Changes:
- Auth0 adapter: treat 404 as "already removed" for all removal ops
  (delete_organization, remove_user_from_organization, delete_invitation)
- remove_member: Auth0 removal first, DB delete second with compensation
- delete_organization: Auth0/Stripe cleanup first, DB delete second
- New set_member_roles across the full stack (protocol, adapter, null/fake)
- New change_member_role service method + PATCH endpoint (Auth0-first)
- sync_user_organizations: now removes stale local memberships not in
  Auth0 and syncs roles on every login
- Admin add-self endpoint: Auth0 add before DB membership write

commit.message:
fix: skip unknown feature flags instead of crashing on org load (#1566)

Removing a FeatureFlag enum member (e.g. s3_destination) while the
database still contains rows with that flag value causes a ValueError
during Pydantic validation of Organization schemas. This breaks every
API request that resolves an org context, returning 422 to all callers.

Gracefully skip unrecognised flags so retiring a feature flag from the
enum no longer requires a coordinated database migration.

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate duplicate data loading in SourceContextBuilder

SourceContextBuilder._get_source_connection_data() duplicated the same
DB queries and registry lookups that SourceLifecycleService already does.
This caused every sync to load source connection + connection rows twice.

Changes:
- Remove _get_source_connection_data (~100 lines) and _create_source_instance
- Builder now calls lifecycle_service.create() directly
- Cursor schema resolved from type(source) instead of separate data load
- Extract snapshot guard into _validate_not_completed_snapshot()
- Simplify get_source_connection_id() to a direct CRUD call
- Remove stale [code blue] comments from builder and search factory
- Drop unused logger param from _load_source_connection_data

fix: resolve 18 test failures introduced by #1507 merge (#1556)

- Add missing Container fields (context_cache, rate_limiter,
  identity_provider, organization_service) to test_container fixture
- Create FakeOrganizationService for test DI
- Set container_mod.container in client fixtures so Inject() resolves

parsing.

- FakeContextCache (read/write/invalidate/assertions)

Lint/type fixes:
- Add D102/D107 docstrings to all branch source files
- Fix F841 unused variable in OrganizationService.get_members
- Add per-file-ignores for tests/fakes in ruff and mypy configs
- Fix mypy type errors in context_resolver, service, repository,
  operations, auth0, logic, analytics, rate_limiter

Note: pre-commit mypy has pre-existing errors in middleware.py,
admin.py, callback_service.py, factory.py — separate cleanup needed.

* fix: address PR #1507 review — Inject DI, protocol inheritance, repo layer cleanup

- Fix Inject() to resolve Container instance instead of module (P0)
- Handle Optional[X] types in Inject cache for fields like ocr_provider
- Fix delete org 400/403 by checking role directly instead of string matching
- Add db.rollback() before fallback user creation in provisioning
- Add explicit Protocol inheritance to all domain repos/services/subscribers
- Fix rate limiter ZSET member collision with unique UUID suffix
- Fix FakeOrganizationRepository.delete to raise on missing org
- Add cache invalidation after org create/delete in lifecycle operations
- Move all raw db calls from provisioning operations into repo layer
- Add create_from_identity, create, refresh to org/user repo protocols

- Update cursor rules for domains/ pattern and Inject(Protocol) DI

- Fix line-too-long violations (E501) in test files
- Restore admin_get_user_principals endpoint removed during refactor
- Add EventSubscriber to core/protocols __all__
- Extract _enrich_from_ctx to reduce track_event complexity (C901)
- Narrow Optional types for mypy (test_logic, test_rate_limiter_plans)
- Use type: ignore for exc.message in test_protocol_exceptions

Note: pre-commit mypy still has pre-existing errors in admin.py,
provisioning/operations.py — separate cleanup needed.

commit.message:
fix: fall back to billing record plan when no current period exists (#1554)

The usage limit checker defaulted to developer plan limits when
get_current_period() returned None (e.g. during a webhook outage
where billing periods go stale). This silently downgraded paid
customers — enterprise orgs hit the 10 source connection cap.

Now falls back to organization_billing.billing_plan before
defaulting to developer, and extracts plan parsing into _parse_plan
which logs invalid values instead of silently swallowing them.

readme_html:
What is Airweave?

Airweave connects to your apps, tools, and databases, continuously syncs their data, and exposes it through a unified, LLM-friendly search interface. AI agents query Airweave to retrieve relevant, grounded, up-to-date context from multiple sources in a single request.

Where it fits

Airweave sits between your data sources and AI systems as shared retrieval infrastructure. It handles authentication, ingestion, syncing, indexing, and retrieval so you don't have to rebuild fragile pipelines for every agent or integration.

How it works

  1. Connect your apps, databases, and documents (50+ integrations)
  2. Airweave syncs, indexes, and exposes your data through a unified retrieval layer
  3. Agents query Airweave via our SDKs, REST API, MCP, or native integrations with popular agent frameworks
  4. Agents retrieve relevant, grounded context on demand

Quickstart

Cloud-hosted: app.airweave.ai

Self-hosted

git clone https://github.com/airweave-ai/airweave.git
cd airweave
./start.sh

→ http://localhost:8080

Requires Docker and docker-compose

Supported Integrations

SDKs

pip install airweave-sdk        # Python
npm install @airweave/sdk       # TypeScript

from airweave import AirweaveSDK

client = AirweaveSDK(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
results = client.collections.search(
    readable_id="my-collection",
    query="Find recent failed payments"
)

Tech Stack

  • Frontend: React/TypeScript with ShadCN
  • Backend: FastAPI (Python)
  • Databases: PostgreSQL (metadata), Vespa (vectors)
  • Workers: Temporal (orchestration), Redis (pub/sub)
  • Deployment: Docker Compose (dev), Kubernetes (prod)

Contributing

We welcome contributions! See our Contributing Guide.

License
